reasoning: The FRED tool returned no data, so a statistical trend analysis wasn't available. Financial data such as yield spreads generally evolve as a random walk in the short term, implying about a 50% probability of an increase over any forward horizon. The most recent data point (Feb 9th) was 0.54%. Without a strong recent trend or data points to anchor on, my expectation defaults to a near coin-flip. I assign 0.51 to the short horizon (allowing for tiny positive drift or mean reversion) and regress to exactly 0.50 for the longer horizon.
